calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; John Cowan appeared by zoom for and with Debra Dolch (Administrator) by zoom.

The Court stated that the only outstanding issue is the request for statutory fees on the increased base of the estate, asked if the parties have any authority for using the re-appraisal for sale value, and stated that there are no schedules of receipts and gains and losses. Mr. Cowan stated that he didn't think a new appraisal was necessary because they had a Court confirmed sale, that the property was listed at $1,608,000.00 and that it sold for $1,688,900.00. Ms. Dolch stated that the schedule was previously filed, that they are not asking that the statutory fees be based on the gains and losses of the sold securities, and that they are asking that it be based on the re-appraisal. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account and for Its Settlement, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Continued To Jan-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Cowan, Esq., counsel for and with Debra Dolch, Executor (appearing by video conference).

The Court recites the matters before the Court, notes in general the panoply of concerns reflected in the Examiner Notes, and notes the recent filings of counsel in response to those Notes. Of immediate concern is that the Proposed Order is not in sufficient form as it does not reflect the exact distributions with specificity, to wit: the specific distribution of securities held in an Arvest Wealth Management account.

Ms. Dolch advises the Court of the need to make distributions before the close of the calendar year due to federal and state estate income tax considerations. The Court advises that in light of the outstanding issues, a general distribution would not be permitted at this time. The Court would, however, consider the partial allowance of fees to reduce taxes to some extent. After discussion, the Court will allow fees in the amount of $20,000 each to counsel and to the Administrator.

The Court continues the matter to January 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (4763 19th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114-2316)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cowan for Petitioner.
Catherine Bubenzerpaim in Propria Persona.
Andreas Bubenzerpaim in Propria Persona.

Mr. Cowan stated that notice of intent to sell was published in the newspaper on January 9th, the 10th and the 16th. Mr. and Mrs. Bubenzerpaim expressed their concerns and stated that the Beneficiary told the Agent several times that she knew she could get more money, but she wanted the property to go to them. The Court stated that it is sympathetic to what is in the pleading that was filed by the Bubenzerpaims, explained that it has to follow the law, that the Administrator has limited authority and that the Court has a duty to ensure that the estate receives the highest value for the property. The sale was confirmed at $1,688,900.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed January 13, 2020. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt of the order and the bond). The amount of the additional bond is to be determined by Counsel.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (4763 19th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114-2316)
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cowan for Petitioner.

The Court stated that the sale can't go forward because there wasn't enough notice and that the notice of hearing needs to be on the Judicial Council form. Mr. Cowan stated that he represents Debra Dolch, that Elizabeth is the Sister and Sole Heir of the Decedent, that Elizabeth was the Administrator before Debra Dolch and that there is no conflict. The Court informed Mr. Cowan that an appraisal on date of death and an appraisal for sale are also needed. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cowan appeared by telephone for Elizabeth Whalley (Petitioner).
Debra Dolch (Fiduciary) also present.

The Court noted that there is a notice issue, that an incorrect form DE-121 was filed at the same time the underlying pleadings were filed which does not comply with the local rules and that an amended notice of hearing form DE-120 was filed maybe as a way to fix that. The Court waived notice at Ms. Dolch's request. The Court also noted that the publication did not include any language regarding IAEA (Independent Administration of Estates Act) powers and informed Mr. Cowan that it is inclined to grant the petition with limited authority. Mr. Cowan stated that he was not sure if it did or not, and that he believes notice is corrected. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration appointing Debra Dolch as the Administrator with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for November 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Siu. Not reported.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-19-2018 Continued To Oct-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cowan for William Thomson.

The Court noted that the Petitioner is asking for no bond. Mr. Cowan stated that the Petitioner is 86 years old and bedridden, that he was a partner to the Decedent for forty years and that the sole heir has nominated him. The Court informed Counsel that bond is needed for the cash assets and that it will only appoint Mr. Thomson with limited authority. The Court also questions whether or not Mr. Thomson will be able to handle administering the estate and raised the issue of having a professional appointed. Mr. Cowan stated that the Petitioner is quite lucid, that the Petitioner has been stuck with a lot of bills and that he will attempt to get a bond. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to see if his Client can qualify for a bond or if he needs to file an amended petition to have a professional appointed.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
